description:
You are a data-analyst who has just received three raw CSV files.  
All three files are already present in  

/home/user/data/raw/  

with the exact names:  

1. sales_north.csv  
2. sales_south.csv  
3. sales_west.csv  

Your manager needs a very quick inventory report that simply states how many CSV files are currently stored in that raw directory.

Your job:

1. Determine, from the shell, how many regular files whose names end in “.csv” are located **directly** inside /home/user/data/raw/ (do not search recursively).
2. Write the result to a brand-new text file located at  

   /home/user/data/report.txt  

   • The file must contain exactly **one** line.  
   • The line must follow this strict format (all capitals, no extra spaces):  

     CSV_COUNT=<NUMBER>  

     where <NUMBER> is the integer count you found.  
   • The file must end with a single newline character.

Important requirements:

• Create /home/user/data/report.txt from the command line (overwrite any existing file of the same name).  
• Do **not** add any additional output, decorative text, headers, or blank lines inside the file.  
• Achieve the entire task with a single shell command.

When the automated test runs, it will read /home/user/data/report.txt and will pass only if:

1. The file exists at the exact path given.  
2. It contains exactly one line.  
3. That line matches the pattern `CSV_COUNT=[0-9]+` (followed by a newline) and the number is correct.
truth:
Pre-existing directory tree and files before the agent starts:

/home/user/data/  
└── raw  
    ├── sales_north.csv  
    ├── sales_south.csv  
    └── sales_west.csv  

No other files with a .csv extension exist in /home/user/data/raw/ at start-up.  
All three files are ordinary regular files (not symlinks or directories).  
Therefore, the correct value that must appear in /home/user/data/report.txt is:

CSV_COUNT=3\n

(Where “\n” denotes the single trailing newline character.)

The automated grader will look for:
• Existence of /home/user/data/report.txt  
• Exact byte contents: 13 bytes total → “C S V _ C O U N T = 3 \n”
